journal:journal.title: Future warming reshapes inorganic nutrient and plankton dynamics in a temperate lake

metadata.description:
Here we provide all codes and datasets used in the associated publication, see the below list (Please see https://github.com/systemsecologygroup/TempSizeMod for a structured directory). 

 

Data processing codes for producing model input include:

  • time-averaged Plankton data (TSM_PlanktonData.py)
  • Mixed layer depth (TSM_MLDForcing.py)
  • PAR (TSM_PARForcing.py)
  • Temperature (TSM_TempForcing.py)

Model codes for producing model output include:

  • Coded model (FinalMod_Sbm.py)
  • Model run script (FinalMod_run.py)
  • Projection script (FinalMod_proj.py)

 

* For access of below files please refer to the previous published version https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.14802352

Model input include:

- Raw data (RawData_XX.csv)

  • Temp+PAR.csv
  • MLD.csv
  • Greifensee size and abundance data of plankton

- Forcing data (ForcData_XX.csv)
(Forcing data are created from RawData, the scripts are provided in https://github.com/systemsecologygroup/TempSizeMod)

  • Lake water surface temperature (LWST)
  • Photosynthetically-active Radiance (PAR)
  • Mixed Layer Depth (MLD); also obtainable from https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3582838

- Plankton data

  • Raw plankton size and abundance (raw_NPdata.csv)
    • time-series data from 13-03-2019 to 31-12-2022
    • contains phosphate concentration (µg/L), phytoplankton abundances, phytoplankton bio-area, zooplankton abundances, zooplankton bio-area, and zooplankton minimum and maximum axis length
  • Transformed plankton size and abundance for model calibrations (PlanktData4Calibr.csv)
    • a composite dataset after transforming plankton abundance to biomass concentration and plankton bio-area to biovolume

 

Model output (results taken from the 10th year run) include:

  • Model results from projection (result_Std+Proj.nc)
  • Model results from sensitivity tests (result_Sensit.nc)

Here contains two sets of model results, one for the projection of future RCP scenarios ('Std+Proj.nc') and one for sensitivity test ('Sensit.nc') over thermal traits (i.e. thermal optima and thermal tolerance) in the standard and RCP scenarios.

 

----------------------- Proj.nc -----------------------
This file stores the output data from the projection, and is saved as a multi-dimensional dataset in a netCDF file. The dataset contains nutrient fluxes ('Nut'), phytoplankton biomass ('Phy'; Pi, i=1,2,..,10) and zooplankton biomass ('Zoo'; Zj, j=1,2) data of each size class from day 3285 to day 3650, on the 10th year of the simulation. This is a time range after the spin-up stage of the model.

To extract the data, one could use the netCDF4 library in python (https://unidata.github.io/netcdf4-python/). Detailed codes are shown in https://github.com/systemsecologygroup/TempSizeMod

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Phy' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'PhySize': '10 phytoplankton size classes ranged from 1 to 200µm ESD',
'RCP': '4 different climate change scenarios considered by IPCC: RCP 2.6; RCP 6.0; and RCP 8.5'

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Zoo' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'ZooSize': '2 zooplankton size classes at 20 and 600µm ESD',
'RCP': '4 different climate change scenarios considered by IPCC: RCP 2.6; RCP 6.0; and RCP 8.5'

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Nut' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'RCP': '4 different climate change scenarios considered by IPCC: RCP 2.6; RCP 6.0; and RCP 8.5'

 

----------------------- Sensit.nc -----------------------
This file stores the output data from the sensitivity test, and is saved as a multi-dimensional dataset in a netCDF file. The dataset contains nutrient fluxes ('Nut'), phytoplankton biomass ('Phy'; Pi, i=1,2,..,10) and zooplankton biomass ('Zoo'; Zj, j=1,2) data of each size class from day 0 to day 3650. For analyses, we used data from the last year, after the model is spinned-up, hence, day 3285 to 3650.

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Phy' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'PhySize': '10 phytoplankton size classes ranged from 1 to 200µm ESD',
'RCP': '4 different climate change scenarios considered by IPCC: RCP 2.6; RCP 6.0; and RCP 8.5',
'Sensit': '4 different configurations on thermal traits to test their sensitivities in climate change scenarios considered by IPCC: RCP 2.6; RCP 6.0; and RCP 8.5'

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Zoo' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'ZooSize': '2 zooplankton size classes at 20 and 600µm ESD',
'RCP': '4 different climate change scenarios considered by IPCC: RCP 2.6; RCP 6.0; and RCP 8.5',
'Sensit': '4 different configurations on thermal traits to test their sensitivities in climate change scenarios considered by IPCC: RCP 2.6; RCP 6.0; and RCP 8.5'

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Nut' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'RCP': '4 different climate change scenarios considered by IPCC: RCP 2.6; RCP 6.0; and RCP 8.5',
'Sensit': '4 different configurations on thermal traits to test their sensitivities in climate change scenarios considered by IPCC: RCP 2.6; RCP 6.0; and RCP 8.5'

 

------------ Sensit_+25%.nc & Sensit_-25%.nc ------------
This file stores the output data from the sensitivity test (for standard model parameters, n=14), and is saved as a multi-dimensional dataset in a netCDF file. The dataset contains nutrient fluxes ('Nut'), phytoplankton biomass ('Phy'; Pi, i=1,2,..,10) and zooplankton biomass ('Zoo'; Zj, j=1,2) data of each size class from day 0 to day 3650. For analyses, we used data from the last year, after the model is spinned-up, hence, day 3285 to 3650.

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Phy' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'PhySize': '10 phytoplankton size classes ranged from 1 to 200µm ESD',
'Parameters': '14 different standard model parameters'

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Zoo' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'ZooSize': '2 zooplankton size classes at 20 and 600µm ESD',
'Parameters': '14 different standard model parameters'

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Nut' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'Parameters': '14 different standard model parameters'

 

--------------------- LakeMix_futMLD.nc ---------------------

This file stores the output data from the sensitivity run for the future MLD (i.e., longer lake stratification period), and is saved as a multi-dimensional dataset in a netCDF file. The run is only conducted for the worst warming scenrio, RCP 8.5. The dataset contains nutrient fluxes ('Nut'), phytoplankton biomass ('Phy'; Pi, i=1,2,..,10) and zooplankton biomass ('Zoo'; Zj, j=1,2) data of each size class from day 3285 to day 3650, on the 10th year of the simulation. This is a time range after the spin-up stage of the model.

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Phy' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'PhySize': '10 phytoplankton size classes ranged from 1 to 200µm ESD',

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Zoo' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'ZooSize': '2 zooplankton size classes at 20 and 600µm ESD',

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Nut' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',

--------------------- LakeMix_k=0.05.nc ---------------------

This file stores the output data from the sensitivity run for a reduced lake diffusive mixing coefficient (k), and is saved as a multi-dimensional dataset in a netCDF file. The run is only conducted for the worst warming scenrio, RCP 8.5. The dataset contains nutrient fluxes ('Nut'), phytoplankton biomass ('Phy'; Pi, i=1,2,..,10) and zooplankton biomass ('Zoo'; Zj, j=1,2) data of each size class from day 3285 to day 3650, on the 10th year of the simulation. This is a time range after the spin-up stage of the model.

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Phy' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'PhySize': '10 phytoplankton size classes ranged from 1 to 200µm ESD',

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Zoo' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days',
'ZooSize': '2 zooplankton size classes at 20 and 600µm ESD',

The coordinates (dimensions) of the 'Nut' dataset are as below:
'time': '365 days'
